text: ScienceDirect (August 2025) examined the integration of Federated Learning, TinyML, and IoT — demonstrating that FL+TinyML pipelines trained across 1,000+ distributed IoT devices (smart home sensors, wearables) achieve within 2-5% accuracy of centralized training while preserving user data privacy, with model sizes compressed to 50-500KB for microcontroller deployment via quantization and knowledge distillation.

Nature Communications (April 2025) addressed the challenge of personalized AI in IoT systems — proposing a framework where federated meta-learning enables rapid adaptation of shared models to individual user behavior patterns (smart home preferences, health monitoring baselines) using just 5-10 local training examples per user, reducing personalization training time by 80% compared to training separate models per device.
